mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.04% as of t-1mo suggests ongoing easing from prior peaks
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 150,000 persons for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,722 thousand as of t-1mo indicates strong employment level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon falls below 0.02 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline exceeds 1.5% or 3-month moving average turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 526,057 million as of t-2mo provides baseline for demand assessment
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization and labor availability. When both rise together, firms expand output and margins, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month growth > 450,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.92 and PAYEMS at 151,722 suggest concurrent supply expansion
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term Rank IC is not significantly higher than univariate INDPRO or PAYEMS IC (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture broad-based demand momentum. When both accelerate, forward-looking equity investors anticipate sustained consumption and investment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 2-month moving average > 1,350 thousand AND RSAFS 2-month moving average > 520,000 million
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,364 thousand and RSAFS at 526,057 million both near recent highs
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration across housing and retail
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term Rank IC does not exceed univariate proxies by at least 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 3.7% signals labor market cooling and reduced wage pressure, which lowers corporate cost expectations and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises 0.3 percentage points or more within a 1-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of t-1mo is near cycle lows, providing room for upward movement
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% signals monetary policy tightening that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.358 and FEDFUNDS at 2.04% indicate potential tightening regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term Rank IC is not significantly negative (correlation > -0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals late-cycle demand exhaustion and reduced corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% or declines 0.5pp from prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,021.86 billion (2017 dollars) as of t-6mo provides baseline for growth assessment
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
